records.ety: [F., a crayon, a lead pencil (crayon Cont Cont's pencil, i. e., one made a black compound invented by Cont), fr. craie chalk, L. creta; said to be, properly, Cretan earth, fr. Creta the island Crete. Cf. Cretaceous.]

defs: An implement for drawing, made of clay and plumbago, or of some preparation of chalk, usually sold in small prisms or cylinders.
quotes: Let no day pass over you . . . without giving some strokes of the pencil or the crayon.
notes: ☞ The black crayon gives a deeper black than the lead pencil. This and the colored crayons are often called chalks. The red crayon is also called sanguine. See Chalk, and Sanguine.

defs: A pencil of carbon used in producing electric light.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Crayoned (-?nd); p. pr. & vb. n. Crayoning.]

defs: To sketch, as with a crayon; to sketch or plan.
quotes: He soon afterwards composed that discourse, conformably to the plan which he had crayoned out.
